system_description.overview: Grove AI develops intelligent AI agents and cloud-based infrastructure to automate complex communication and data workflows in regulated healthcare environments. The platform combines generative AI, structured data orchestration, and multichannel communication to enable high-scale, protocol-aligned engagement across patients, providers, and care teams.

products.Grove AI PRM: Cloud-based platform for managing AI agents, querying and organizing interaction data, and tracking operational workflows. Provides real-time dashboards, APIs, and structured data pipelines adaptable to clinical, commercial, or operational use cases.
products.Grove AI Agents: AI-powered virtual staff capable of engaging participants across voice, SMS, email, and chat. Automate tasks like intake, triage, follow-ups, data collection, and appointment scheduling based on user-defined workflows.
products.Support & Services: Onboarding, configuration, engineering support, integrations, compliance support, and customer success guidance.

regions: Region 3 (3 GCP regions shown in network diagram)
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; no physical servers. All data processed on cloud services.

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in GCP VPC environment with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation separates systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Network Endpoint Groups (shown in network diagram)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via GCP GuardDuty to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS (Transport Layer Security) using certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> Load Balancer -> Network Endpoint Groups -> Main Servers (Grove PRM in 3 regions) -> Users and Services. Production servers maintain failover capabilities across multiple availability zones.

application_architecture.pattern: Hybrid (multi-region cloud-native with microservices-style Grove PRM deployment across 3 regions)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested in dev/test before production

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: GCP-managed (passwords controlled through GCP Password policy); Active Directory mentioned for server/database accounts
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code tool access; 2FA required for employee email access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: GCP Password policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry, and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (GCP KMS)
encryption.key_management: GCP KMS (cloud provider key management service); access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool with branch rules (specific tool not named; GitHub/GitLab style based on description)
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not explicitly named; automated deployment pipeline implied
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request requires additional approval prior to merging to master branch
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as code; configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned for patch compliance daily; vulnerability scans monthly

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tool (specific product not named) continuously monitors system capacity and performance; generates alerts when predefined thresholds met
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled at application and infrastructure level; access to change log configuration and modify logs is restricted
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events; tracked in change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Data center services, cloud infrastructure, IDS/IPS via GuardDuty

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers and Mac-based workstations/laptops; auto-scan on continuous basis; virus definitions updated weekly on production servers, daily on endpoints
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; internal IT Infrastructure team performs scans
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party

untestable: CC2.2.3 - No significant changes to people/roles during engagement period
CC3.2.1 (cybersecurity insurance) - No cyber incidents reported during engagement period
CC6.5.2 - No customer terminations during engagement period
CC7.3.1 - No security incidents reported during engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical security delegated to GCP as subservice organization
A1.2 - Environmental security managed by building management/GCP; Grove AI has no office
notable_controls: Multi-region architecture with 3 GCP regions for high availability
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
BCDR tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) with quarterly meetings

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-region (3 GCP regions), multi-AZ, IaC for baseline configurations, VPC with segmentation, failover capability. No CDN mentioned and cloud provider not fully named in specifics, but architecture is strong.

application_architecture.rationale: Multi-region deployment of Grove PRM, separate environments, branch protection, code review required. No programming languages or frameworks disclosed. Architecture appears well-structured but limited technical detail.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups, multi-AZ, data classification and retention policies, per-tenant segregation. Specific DB technologies not named. No caching layer or data warehouse mentioned.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen testing, monthly vulnerability scans, IDS/IPS via GCP GuardDuty, firewalls, MFA enforced, anti-malware on all endpoints and servers, cybersecurity insurance, encrypted data at rest and in transit.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC for configuration management, branch protection with approval gates, separate environments, emergency change process, automated patch management. CI/CD tool not explicitly named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually; tested annually for multiple loss scenarios; multi-AZ and multi-region failover; daily backups. RTO/RPO not disclosed.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavily GCP-dependent with GCP as sole named subservice organization. No other cloud vendors mentioned. Single critical infrastructure dependency.

overall.rationale: Grove AI demonstrates a mature security posture for an early-stage healthcare AI company: multi-region GCP, IDS/IPS, annual pen testing, BCDR testing, MFA, quarterly access reviews, and clean SOC 2 Type 2 audit. Key gaps are single cloud provider dependency and limited disclosure of specific application technology stack.

red_flags: Single cloud provider dependency on GCP with no secondary provider or DR cloud mentioned
No WAF mentioned despite web-based application serving healthcare data
Application technology stack (languages, frameworks, databases) not disclosed
yellow_flags: RTO and RPO not disclosed despite BCDR plan existing
IDS/IPS attributed to 'GCP GuardDuty' - GuardDuty is an AWS service; GCP equivalent is Security Command Center/Cloud IDS - possible template error in report
No CDN mentioned despite multichannel communication platform with voice/SMS/email
Source code management tool not explicitly named
green_flags: Full SOC 2 Type 2 with Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy trust criteria - all 5 criteria
Clean audit: zero exceptions across all tested controls
Multi-region architecture (3 GCP regions) with multi-AZ failover
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
BCDR plan tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Access revoked within 1 business day of termination
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) providing board-level security oversight
key_observations: Grove AI is a healthcare-focused AI company (regulated environment) that has achieved SOC 2 Type 2 across all 5 trust criteria - notable for an early-stage company
The report uses 'GCP GuardDuty' which appears to be a template error (GuardDuty is AWS); likely refers to GCP Security Command Center or Cloud IDS
Network diagram shows 3-region GCP deployment with load balancer and Network Endpoint Groups - suggesting significant investment in availability architecture
No physical office mentioned; fully cloud-native operation
Company signed by CTO (Sohit Gatiganti) rather than CEO, indicating technology-led organization
Accorp Partners CPA LLC (Kalispell, Montana) is a smaller regional audit firm - not a Big 4 or top-tier SOC auditor
